message:
fix: Correct UTF-8 handling in OSC 8 URL escaping

The previous implementation used `byte as char` which incorrectly
interprets high bytes (0x80-0xFF) as Latin-1 characters instead of
preserving them as raw UTF-8 bytes. This corrupted URLs containing
non-ASCII characters like Japanese, emoji, etc.

Fixed by:
- Working with byte vectors instead of String::push(char)
- Preserving all non-control bytes unchanged
- Using uppercase hex (%1B instead of %1b) for RFC 3986 compatibility
- Added tests for Unicode URL preservation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
